faqs.title: How Long Does It Take to Build a Website?
faqs.directAnswer: Building a website typically takes 4–12 weeks: a simple custom site around 4–6 weeks, a complex site or web application 3–6 months, and a large platform or SaaS product longer. Timeline depends on scope, complexity, content readiness, and feedback speed. A clear scope and prompt feedback keep projects on the faster end.

faqs.title: Is It Cheaper to Build or Buy a Website?
faqs.directAnswer: For simple needs, buying (a template or website builder) is cheaper and faster. For sites that must convert, integrate, differentiate, or scale, building custom costs more but returns it through capability a template can
